page: Amazon Web Services m8ib.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor architecture, which offloads virtualization functions to dedicated hardware.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 67 Gbps.

Due to the lack of local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, this instance is not designed for local-disk-intensive databases or hardware-accelerated machine learning workloads. Instead, its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and high network throughput make it appropriate for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, application servers, and enterprise software deployments that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8ib.16xlarge is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors, it provides 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz, alongside 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is designed for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources, such as enterprise applications, web serving, and containerized deployments, without the need for local high-speed disk access or specialized graphics processing.
